mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0% signals labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 8.0% in consecutive monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both HOUST and PAYEMS rise concurrently for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising federal funds rates increase discount rates and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL above 3.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects stronger manufacturing output and business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when INDPRO increases month-over-month for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales surging past 400 billion dollars signals robust consumer demand, lifting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when RSAFS crosses above 400000 in monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid job creation combined with a sharp drop in unemployment accelerates wage growth and consumption, boosting equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously over a 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of near-zero interest rates, further rate cuts provide diminishing marginal support to equities, causing S&P 500 returns to stagnate or decline over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for three consecutive months.
